strategy: Attack with Myth (he takes +30% Myth); never Storm (51% resist) and expect 21% resist on the other schools.
Set up fully before you swing. Any all-enemy or targeted attack makes him cheat-cast Hamadryad, healing every enemy ~800 and cleansing their DOTs, unless the attack defeats him.
Focus the Sultan and one-shot him. The heal does not fire if that attack kills the Iron Sultan, so line up a bladed kill hit rather than chipping.
Protect your Feint with Indemnity. Feinting him makes him Cleanse Ward twice and strip his harmful wards, wiping the Feint.
Account for the Gold Dragon's round-one Shadow Sentinel (75% intercept), which soaks and redirects hits, so clear the Dragon or plan around it.
Top up the lowest-Health wizard before Round 5. He casts +35% Darkwind at the end of every fifth Round, then immediately hits the lowest-Health wizard with Iron Sultan. Do not join late.
cheatNotes: A wizard who joins late gets hit with Iron Sultan (Shadow) immediately, so enter on time.
On Round 1 the Gold Dragon gives itself a Shadow Sentinel with 75% intercept, 100 Critical Block, and -30% incoming, so it soaks and redirects hits; clear it or plan around the intercept.
Any all-enemy or targeted-enemy attack makes him cheat-cast Hamadryad, healing every enemy ~800 and cleansing their DOTs, so set up fully and only swing when you can kill him.
That heal does not fire if the attack defeats the Iron Sultan, so one-shot him to skip it entirely.
Feinting him makes him Cleanse Ward twice and strip all his harmful wards, wiping your Feint, so protect it with Indemnity.
At the end of every fifth Round he casts a +35% Darkwind global.
Right after that Darkwind he hits the lowest-Health wizard with Iron Sultan, so heal that wizard above the danger line before Round 5 ends.
